function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a file's contents. Returns line-numbered output. For large files, use start_line/end_line to read specific sections.

end_line.description: End line (1-indexed, inclusive). Omit to read to end.

start_line.description: Start line (1-indexed). Omit to read from beginning.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file

paths.description: Array of file paths to read (max 10)

function.name: batch_read_files
function.description: Read multiple files at once. More efficient than multiple read_file calls. Returns contents of up to 10 files.

path.description: Absolute or relative path of the file to write

content.description: The full content to write to the file

function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file and parent directories if they don't exist. Overwrites existing content. For targeted changes to existing files, prefer edit_file.
function.name: write_file
edit_file
function.description: Make a targeted edit to a file by finding exact old content and replacing it with new content. Preserves the rest of the file. The old_content must match exactly (including whitespace and indentation). Always read_file first to see exact content.

path.description: Path to the file to edit

old_content.description: The exact content to find (must match file contents exactly)

function.name: create_file
function.description: Create a new file. Fails if the file already exists (use write_file to overwrite). Creates parent directories automatically.

function.name: delete_file
function.description: Delete a file or empty directory.
Move or rename a file or directory. Creates parent directories of destination if needed.
function.name: move_file

function.description: Copy a file or directory to a new location. Use for backups or duplicating files. Does not overwrite by default; set overwrite true to replace.
function.name: copy_file

path.description: Path to the file or directory

function.name: file_info
function.description: Get metadata for a file or directory: size, is_directory, modification date, extension. Use to check existence and stats before reading or editing.
Check if a path exists and whether it is a file or directory. Returns exists, is_directory.
function.name: path_exists
count_lines
function.description: Count lines in a text file. Fast way to get file size in lines without reading content. Useful for deciding whether to use read_file with ranges.
List files and directories with sizes. Supports recursive listing. Automatically hides .git, node_modules, and dotfiles unless show_hidden is true.
function.name: list_directory
tree_view
function.description: Show a tree view of a directory structure. Automatically excludes .git, node_modules, __pycache__, .build directories. Great for understanding project layout.

path.description: Root directory to show tree for

function.name: search_files
function.description: Search for files by name pattern within a directory tree. Uses glob patterns. Excludes .git and node_modules.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match file names (e.g. '*.swift', 'package.json')

function.name: grep_search

is_regex.description: Treat query as regex (default: false, uses literal/fixed string matching)

path.description: Directory or file to search within

function.description: Search for text patterns in file contents across a directory. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Excludes .git, node_modules, .build directories.

function.name: find_and_replace
function.description: Find and replace a string across all files in a directory. Useful for renaming symbols, updating imports, or project-wide refactoring. Supports dry_run to preview changes.

dry_run.description: Preview changes without modifying files (default: false)

function.description: Append content to an existing file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist. Does not overwrite.
function.name: append_file

function.description: Create a directory (and parent directories if needed). Fails if path exists and is not a directory.
function.name: create_directory

function.description: Compress files or a directory into a zip archive.
function.name: compress_files
extract_archive
function.description: Extract a zip or tar archive to a directory.

function.name: run_command
function.description: Execute a shell command (via zsh) and return stdout, stderr, and exit code. Use for builds, tests, git operations, package management, and any CLI task. Default timeout is 60 seconds.

cwd.description: Working directory for the command (defaults to project working directory if set)

function.name: run_background
function.description: Run a command in the background. Returns immediately with PID. Use kill_process to stop.
Kill a process by PID. Sends SIGTERM by default; use signal 9 for SIGKILL.
function.name: kill_process
which
function.description: Find the path of an executable in PATH.

function.name: system_run
function.description: Execute a shell command with system-level exec approvals. Use for running binaries outside the project (e.g. system tools, Homebrew). Subject to user allowlist and approval prompts. Prefer run_command for project-scoped builds and scripts.

function.name: system_notify
function.description: Show a native system notification to the user. Use for alerts, completion notices, or reminders.
Read environment variable(s). Pass name for one variable, or omit to get all (filtered to common dev vars). Use to check PATH, HOME, LANG, or project-specific vars.

name.description: Optional. Variable name (e.g. PATH, HOME). If omitted, returns a summary of common env vars.

function.name: get_env

function.description: Get the current working directory (project root). Use to verify paths or when paths must be resolved relative to the project.
function.name: get_cwd
list_env
function.description: List all environment variables as key=value pairs. Use to inspect PATH, LANG, project env, or debug environment issues.

prefix.description: Optional. Only list vars whose names start with this prefix (e.g. 'PATH', 'NODE').

function.name: list_processes

filter.description: Optional. Substring to filter process names (e.g. 'node', 'xcode').

function.description: List running processes. Optional filter by name substring (e.g. 'node', 'python'). Returns PID, command, and optionally CPU/memory. Useful to see what's running before starting servers or debugging.

function.name: disk_usage

path.description: Optional. Path to check (directory or file). Defaults to working directory.

function.description: Get disk usage for a path or the project directory. Returns total, used, free space in human-readable form. Useful to check space before large operations.

function.name: clipboard_read
function.description: Read the current string content from the system clipboard.

text.description: The text to copy to the clipboard

function.name: clipboard_write
function.description: Write a string to the system clipboard.
Open a URL in the default browser or application (e.g. https://, file://, mailto:).
function.name: open_url
open_app
function.description: Open an application by name or bundle identifier (macOS).

name_or_bundle_id.description: Application name (e.g. Safari, Xcode) or bundle id (e.g. com.apple.Safari)

function.name: screen_snapshot
function.description: Capture a single screenshot of the screen. Returns the path to the saved PNG file. Requires Screen Recording permission on macOS.

function.name: screen_record
function.description: Start a short screen recording (macOS). Saves to a temp file and returns the path. Requires Screen Recording permission. Duration in seconds (max 60).

function.name: camera_snap
function.description: Capture a single still image from the camera (macOS). Returns path to the saved image. Requires Camera permission.

function.name: window_list
function.description: List visible windows (macOS). Requires Accessibility permission. Returns app names and window titles.
Capture an accessibility snapshot of the frontmost window or a named app (macOS). Requires Accessibility permission. Returns a text description of the UI hierarchy.

app_name.description: Optional: target app by name (e.g. Safari). If omitted, uses frontmost window.

function.name: window_snapshot

function.description: Search the web for information. Returns titles, snippets, and URLs. Use for documentation lookups, error troubleshooting, finding API references, and current information.
function.name: web_search

query.description: The search query (be specific for better results)

function.description: Fetch and read a web page. HTML is stripped to plain text for readability. Use after web_search to read specific results, or for documentation pages.
function.name: read_url
fetch_json
function.description: Fetch a URL and parse the response as JSON. Returns the parsed structure as text.
Download a file from a URL and save it to a local path.

path.description: Local path to save the file

function.name: download_file

function.description: View the structural outline of a source code file — functions, classes, structs, imports, etc. Language-aware patterns for Swift, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Rust, Go, and Java.

path.description: Path to the source code file

function.name: view_code_outline

command.description: Optional. Override build command (e.g. 'npm run build', 'xcodebuild'). If omitted, inferred from project files.

function.name: run_build
function.description: Run the project's build. Infers command from package.json (npm run build), Package.swift (swift build), Cargo.toml (cargo build), Makefile (make), or pass a custom command. Use after editing code to verify it compiles.
Run the project's code formatter (prettier, swiftformat, black, rustfmt, etc.).

path.description: Optional. Path or file to format.

function.name: run_format
get_package_deps
function.description: List project dependencies from package.json, Package.swift, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, etc.
Run npm install to add or update dependencies. Use package to add a specific package.
function.name: npm_install

package.description: Optional. Package name to add (e.g. lodash).

function.name: pip_install

package.description: Package to install (e.g. requests). Omit to install from requirements.txt.

function.description: Run pip install to add Python packages.
Add a Rust dependency with cargo add.
function.name: cargo_add

function.description: Run the project's linter or formatter. Tries eslint, swiftlint, ruff, clippy, etc. from project config, or use optional command override. Use to check code quality after edits.

path.description: Optional. Path or file to lint (default: project root).

function.name: run_linter

function.description: Run the project's test suite. Detects test command from package.json (npm test), Package.swift (swift test), pyproject.toml (pytest), Cargo.toml (cargo test), or use an optional command override.

command.description: Optional. Override the test command (e.g. 'npm test', 'cargo test', 'pytest'). If omitted, the command is inferred from project files.

function.name: run_tests

function.description: Run git status and optionally git diff --stat in the working directory. Use to see what files changed, branch, and staged/unstaged state before or after edits.
function.name: git_status

include_diff_stat.description: If true, also run git diff --stat to show changed line counts. Default true.

function.name: git_log

path.description: Optional. Limit log to this file or path.

function.description: Show git commit log. Optional limit and path filter. Use to see recent history or who changed a file.

ref.description: Optional. Compare against ref (e.g. HEAD~1, main).

path.description: Optional. Limit diff to this file or path.

function.description: Show git diff. Optional: staged only, or between refs, or for a specific path. Use to see exact changes before/after edits.
function.name: git_diff

function.description: List git branches. Shows current branch and optionally all remotes. Use to see branch structure and current branch.
function.name: git_branch

function.description: Show a file at a given git ref (commit, branch, or tag). Use to see previous version of a file or compare.

ref.description: Commit, branch, or tag (e.g. HEAD, main, abc123)

path.description: Path to the file in the repo

function.name: git_show
git_add
function.description: Stage files for commit.

paths.description: Paths to stage. Use . for all.

function.name: git_commit
function.description: Create a commit with staged changes.
Stash or pop changes. Use action: push to stash, pop to restore.
function.name: git_stash
git_checkout
function.description: Checkout a branch or restore files.

paths.description: Optional. Paths to restore (use with target: --)

function.description: Push commits to remote.
function.name: git_push

function.description: Pull from remote.
function.name: git_pull
get_system_info
function.description: Get system information (OS, architecture, hostname).
List network interfaces and their status.
function.name: list_network_interfaces
sqlite_query
function.description: Execute a SQL query on an SQLite database file. Returns results as CSV. Use SELECT queries only; for schema inspection use sqlite_schema or sqlite_tables.

function.name: sqlite_schema
function.description: Get the schema (CREATE TABLE statements) of an SQLite database. Optionally filter by table name.

table.description: Optional: filter to a specific table

function.name: sqlite_tables
function.description: List all tables in an SQLite database. Returns table names.

function.name: image_info
function.description: Get metadata for an image file: dimensions, format, file size. Supports PNG, JPEG, GIF, HEIC, WebP, etc.
Resize an image. Creates a new file or overwrites. Specify max width or height to scale proportionally, or both for exact dimensions. macOS only.
function.name: image_resize

height.description: Exact height (with width for exact resize)

width.description: Exact width (with height for exact resize)

function.description: Convert an image to another format (e.g. PNG to JPEG, HEIC to PNG). macOS only.
function.name: image_convert

method.description: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE (default: GET)

function.name: http_request
function.description: Make an HTTP request (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE). Returns response body and status code. Use for REST APIs.

function.name: read_env_file
function.description: Read a .env file and return key-value pairs. Handles KEY=value format.

path.description: Path to .env file (default: .env in working directory)

function.description: Write or update a .env file. Merges with existing or creates new. Keys are uppercased.
function.name: write_env_file

all.description: Show all containers including stopped (default: true)

function.name: docker_ps
function.description: List Docker containers. Equivalent to docker ps -a. Requires Docker.

function.name: docker_images
function.description: List Docker images. Equivalent to docker images. Requires Docker.

function.name: get_current_time
function.description: Get the current date and time in ISO 8601 format and timezone.

date.description: Input date string (ISO 8601 or common formats)

format.description: Output format: iso, short, long, unix

function.description: Format a date string. Parse input and output in specified format.
function.name: format_date
calculate
function.description: Evaluate a math expression. Supports +, -, *, /, %, ^, sqrt, etc.

function.name: count_words

path.description: Path to file to count (alternative to text)

text.description: Text to count (or use path for file)

function.description: Count words, lines, and characters in a text string or file.
Extract all URLs from a text string or file.

path.description: Path to file to extract URLs from

function.name: extract_urls

function.description: Parse JSON string and return formatted/validated output. Use to inspect JSON structure.
function.name: json_parse
yaml_parse
function.description: Parse YAML string. Uses Python yaml or ruby -r yaml if available.

function.name: diff_files
function.description: Show diff between two files. Uses diff command.
Compute hash of a file (MD5 or SHA256).
function.name: file_hash

function.description: Create a backup copy of a file with .bak or timestamp suffix.
function.name: backup_file
git_remote
function.description: List or show git remotes.
List git tags.
function.name: git_tag
git_reset
function.description: Reset git working tree. Use soft/mixed/hard.
Ping a host to check connectivity.
function.name: ping_host
resolve_dns
function.description: Resolve hostname to IP address(es).

function.name: hash_string
function.description: Compute hash of a string (MD5, SHA256).

function.name: base64_encode
function.description: Encode a string to Base64. Use for encoding credentials or binary-like data for APIs.

function.name: base64_decode
function.description: Decode a Base64 string. Use to decode API responses or stored credentials.
Generate a UUID (Universally Unique Identifier).
function.name: generate_uuid
get_file_type
function.description: Get file type/extension and MIME type if determinable.
Detect programming language of a file from extension and/or content.
function.name: detect_language

function.description: Get process details by PID (command, args, status). macOS/Linux.
function.name: get_process_info

function.description: Run a Docker container from an image. Returns container ID and output.

command.description: Optional command to run inside the container

function.name: docker_run

function.description: Build a Docker image from a Dockerfile.

dockerfile.description: Path to Dockerfile (if not in context root)

path.description: Path to the build context (directory with Dockerfile)

tag.description: Tag for the built image (e.g. myapp:latest)

function.name: docker_build

tail.description: Number of lines from the end (default 100)

function.description: Get logs from a running or stopped Docker container.
function.name: docker_logs
docker_compose_up
function.description: Start services defined in docker-compose.yml.

function.name: docker_compose_down
function.description: Stop and remove docker-compose services.

output.description: Output format: wide, json, yaml (default: wide)

resource.description: Resource type (pods, services, deployments, nodes, etc.)

function.description: Get Kubernetes resources (pods, services, deployments, etc.).
function.name: kubectl_get

path.description: Path to manifest file or directory

function.name: kubectl_apply
function.description: Apply a Kubernetes manifest file or directory.

function.name: browser_open
function.description: Open a URL in the default web browser.
Capture a screenshot of a webpage using a headless browser. Returns the image path.
function.name: browser_screenshot

function.description: Load a URL and evaluate JavaScript in a headless browser context. Returns the JS evaluation result.

wait_ms.description: Wait time after page load before evaluating (ms, default 1000)

function.name: browser_evaluate
generate_embeddings
function.description: Generate vector embeddings for text using a language model. Useful for semantic search.
Search files by meaning using embeddings. Finds semantically similar code or text across a directory.
function.name: semantic_search
summarize_text
function.description: Summarize a long text or file contents into a concise summary.

function.name: vercel_deploy
function.description: Deploy a project to Vercel using the Vercel CLI.
Get deployment logs from Vercel.
function.name: vercel_logs
netlify_deploy

site_id.description: Netlify site ID (uses linked site if omitted)

function.description: Deploy a site to Netlify using the Netlify CLI.

function.name: fly_deploy
function.description: Deploy an application to Fly.io.

image.description: Docker image to deploy (if not building)

function.name: regex_replace
function.description: Find and replace using regular expressions across one or more files.

function.name: port_scan
function.description: Check if a TCP port is open on a host.
Check SSL/TLS certificate details for a hostname. Returns issuer, expiry, validity.
function.name: ssl_check

count.description: Number of next run times to show (default 5)

function.description: Parse a cron expression and show the next N scheduled run times.
function.name: cron_parse
json_schema_validate
function.description: Validate a JSON document against a JSON Schema.

json.description: JSON string or file path to validate

schema.description: JSON Schema string or file path

function.description: Parse a source file into an abstract syntax tree and return its symbol outline (classes, functions, properties, imports). Supports Swift, TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, Go, and Rust. Returns a structured JSON tree of symbols with their types, line numbers, and nesting.
function.name: ast_parse

depth.description: Maximum nesting depth to return (default: all levels)

language.description: Language hint (swift, typescript, python, go, rust). Auto-detected from extension if omitted.

path.description: Path to the source file to parse

symbol.description: The symbol name to search for (e.g., 'sendMessage', 'ChatViewModel', 'UserModel')

language.description: Filter to specific language files (swift, typescript, python, etc.)

include_definitions.description: Include the definition site in results (default: true)

path.description: Directory or file to search within (defaults to working directory)

function.name: find_references
function.description: Find all references to a symbol (function, class, variable, type) across the project. Uses ripgrep-based search with language-aware pattern matching. Returns file paths, line numbers, and surrounding context for each reference.

function.name: type_check
function.description: Run the language's type checker or compiler in check mode and return diagnostics (errors, warnings). Supports: swift build (Swift), tsc --noEmit (TypeScript), mypy (Python), go vet (Go), cargo check (Rust). Returns structured diagnostics with file, line, severity, and message.

path.description: Project directory or specific file to type-check

language.description: Language (swift, typescript, python, go, rust). Auto-detected if omitted.

strict.description: Enable strict mode where available (default: false)

function.name: dependency_graph

format.description: Output format: 'json' (adjacency list), 'tree' (text tree), or 'dot' (graphviz). Default: json

depth.description: Maximum depth of transitive dependencies to include (default: 2)

function.description: Build and return the dependency graph of a project. Shows which files/modules import which others. Supports Package.swift, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod. Returns a JSON adjacency list of module dependencies.

threshold.description: Complexity threshold above which functions are flagged (default: 10)

language.description: Language filter (swift, typescript, python, etc.). Auto-detected if omitted.

function.name: code_complexity
function.description: Calculate cyclomatic complexity and other code metrics for functions in a file or directory. Reports complexity score, lines of code, nesting depth, and parameter count per function. Flags functions exceeding threshold as needing refactoring.

query.description: Search query (supports natural language and metadata filters like 'kind:pdf')

directory.description: Optional directory to scope the search to

function.description: Search files, emails, messages, and content on the local Mac using Spotlight (CoreSpotlight/mdfind). Returns file paths, types, and metadata.
function.name: spotlight_search

key.description: The key/account name to look up

function.description: Read a value from the macOS Keychain. Only reads items created by G-Rump (service: com.grump.*). Returns the stored string value.
function.name: keychain_read

function.description: Store a key-value pair in the macOS Keychain under G-Rump's service. Use for API keys, tokens, or secrets that should persist securely.

key.description: The key/account name to store under

function.name: keychain_store
calendar_events
function.description: List or create calendar events using EventKit. Can query events in a date range or create new events.

action.description: 'list' to query events, 'create' to add a new event

function.description: List or create reminders using EventKit. Query incomplete reminders or add new ones with optional due dates.
function.name: reminders_list

action.description: 'list' to query reminders, 'create' to add a new reminder

function.description: Search the macOS Contacts (address book) by name, email, or phone number. Returns matching contact info.

query.description: Search query (name, email, or phone number)

function.name: contacts_search

function.description: Transcribe an audio file to text using Apple's Speech framework. Supports WAV, M4A, MP3, CAF formats. Runs entirely on-device.
function.name: speech_transcribe
ocr_extract
function.description: Extract text from an image using Apple's Vision framework (VNRecognizeTextRequest). Runs entirely on-device. Supports PNG, JPEG, TIFF, HEIC.

function.name: image_classify
function.description: Classify image contents using Apple's Vision framework (VNClassifyImageRequest). Returns labels with confidence scores. Runs on-device.

function.name: shortcuts_run
function.description: Run a named macOS Shortcut via the 'shortcuts' CLI. Can pass input text and receive output.

name.description: Name of the Shortcut to run

input.description: Optional input text to pass to the Shortcut

function.name: system_appearance
function.description: Get or set macOS system appearance settings. Can read current dark/light mode, accent color, and highlight color.

action.description: 'get' to read current appearance, 'set' to change it

dark_mode.description: Set dark mode on/off (only for action=set)

workspace.description: Path to .xcworkspace file (use instead of project)

function.description: Build Xcode projects or workspaces. Reports build errors, warnings, and test results. Supports build, test, clean, and archive actions.
function.name: xcodebuild
xcrun_simctl
function.description: Manage iOS/watchOS/tvOS simulators via xcrun simctl. List, boot (bootstatus waits until usable), shutdown, install/launch/terminate apps, read an app's recent logs, take screenshots — the same loop the Run button drives.

device_id.description: Simulator device UUID (required for everything except list)

function.description: Format Swift source files using swift-format. Applies consistent style rules.
function.name: swift_format

in_place.description: Modify files in place (default: false, prints to stdout)

function.name: swift_lint
function.description: Run SwiftLint on Swift source files. Returns violations with file, line, severity, and rule name.

function.name: swift_package
function.description: Run Swift Package Manager commands: resolve, update, show-dependencies, generate-xcodeproj, dump-package.
Extract text content from a PDF file using PDFKit. Returns text from all pages or a specific page range.
function.name: pdf_extract
tts_speak
function.description: Speak text aloud using AVSpeechSynthesizer. Uses system voices. Can also save speech to audio file.

output_path.description: Optional path to save audio file instead of speaking

function.description: Generate a QR code image from text using CoreImage CIQRCodeGenerator. Saves as PNG.
function.name: qr_generate

content.description: Text or URL to encode in the QR code

output_path.description: Path to save the QR code PNG image

function.name: websocket_send
function.description: Connect to a WebSocket endpoint, send a message, and return the response. Uses URLSessionWebSocketTask.

headers.description: JSON string of additional headers (e.g. authorization)

function.name: graphql_query
function.description: Execute a GraphQL query or mutation against an endpoint. Returns JSON response.
Discover local network services using Bonjour/mDNS (NetServiceBrowser). Finds services like web servers, printers, AirPlay devices.
function.name: bonjour_discover

function.description: Search Apple Developer Documentation for APIs, frameworks, guides, and sample code. Returns structured results with titles, types, summaries, and URLs from developer.apple.com.
function.name: apple_docs_search

query.description: Search query (e.g. 'SwiftUI NavigationStack', 'URLSession async', 'Core Data migration')

function.description: Get current SourceKit-LSP diagnostics (errors and warnings) for the workspace. Returns structured compiler errors with file, line, column, severity, and message.

file.description: Optional: filter diagnostics to a specific file path. Omit to get all diagnostics.

function.name: lsp_diagnostics

directory.description: Directory to scan (defaults to working directory)

function.name: accessibility_audit
function.description: Scan Swift files in the workspace for accessibility issues: missing accessibility labels, small touch targets, hardcoded font sizes, missing Dynamic Type support, and poor contrast patterns.

directory.description: Directory to scan (defaults to working directory)

function.name: localization_audit
function.description: Scan Swift files for hardcoded user-facing strings that should use NSLocalizedString or String(localized:). Also reports .xcstrings/.strings coverage and missing translations.

function.name: spm_resolve
function.description: Resolve or update Swift Package Manager dependencies. Parses Package.swift and Package.resolved to report dependency versions, then optionally runs 'swift package resolve' or 'swift package update'.
Run App Store submission pre-flight checks: verify app icons, privacy manifest, Info.plist keys, entitlements, and deployment targets. Returns a pass/fail/warning checklist.
function.name: app_store_checklist

directory.description: Project directory to check (defaults to working directory)

function.description: Present a question to the user with multiple-choice options. The user will see a clickable grid of option cards (A, B, C, D format) and can select their answer with a single click. Use this whenever you need the user to choose between specific options.
function.name: ask_user

options.description: 2-4 options for the user to choose from

label.description: Short label for the option (e.g. 'Option A')

description.description: Longer description of what this option means

question.description: The question to ask the user

scope.description: 'project' (default when a project is open) or 'global'

text.description: The lesson, imperative, max 280 chars (e.g. 'Run make generate after editing project.yml in this repo')

keywords.description: Trigger keywords — the lesson surfaces when a request mentions them

function.description: Save a durable lesson learned during this run (e.g. a pitfall to avoid, a project convention, a tool quirk). Lessons are injected into future runs when relevant, tracked for accuracy, and visible to the user in the Learning panel. Keep it to one imperative sentence.
function.name: record_lesson

function.description: Store a fact in persistent memory (hybrid vector + keyword recall in future conversations). Use for durable facts about the project or user — not for lessons about your own behavior (use record_lesson for those).
function.name: remember

function.description: Run a reflection pass over the most recent completed run: distill durable lessons, adjust existing lesson confidence, and (rarely) propose a skill for user approval. Use when you notice a pattern worth keeping, or when the user asks you to learn from what just happened.
function.name: reflect

focus.description: Optional: what to pay attention to (e.g. 'the build failures')

function.description: Propose a new or updated SKILL.md for the user to review. The proposal appears in the Learning panel as a diff and does NOTHING until the user approves it — never assume it was accepted. Requires at least 3 recorded lessons clustering around one workflow (cite their ids).
function.name: propose_skill

description.description: One-line description (when to use the skill)

lesson_ids.description: ≥3 lesson ids this skill is distilled from

body.description: What to do and how to verify it

function.description: Queue a goal for the autonomous daemon (vault Goals/ folder). The daemon works pending goals on a scratch branch when enabled, with every write approval-gated. Use for follow-up work that shouldn't block the current conversation.
function.name: add_goal
